message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): empty-PEM error-variant contracts (tick 336)

Added two tests pinning error-variant fidelity for PEM files that are
syntactically valid but semantically wrong — a common operator
failure mode when copy-pasting certificates or swapping cert/key files.

- `build_tls_bundle_surfaces_empty_cert_chain_for_empty_pem_file` —
  a file containing only PEM comments (no `-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----`
  block) as cert_path surfaces `EmptyCertChain(path)` with the offending
  file path, not a generic Config(_) or Io(_).

- `build_tls_bundle_surfaces_empty_private_key_for_cert_in_key_slot` —
  a valid server cert PEM mistakenly pointed at by `key_path` (the
  classic "cert and key swapped" mis-config) surfaces
  `EmptyPrivateKey(path)`. rustls's key parser doesn't find key blocks
  in a file containing only cert blocks, so this variant fires cleanly.

Why this variant fidelity matters: caller-side error routing
distinguishes "file exists but wrong shape" (operator-actionable:
re-paste the correct PEM contents) from "file missing" (operator-
actionable: check deployment) from "rustls-internal config error"
(bug-report-actionable). A regression that collapsed the empty-PEM
cases into Config(_) would erase the distinction between "wrong file
pasted" and "library bug".

Together with ticks 333-335, now covers 6 of the 8 DistributedTlsError
variants via round-trip tests of `build_tls_bundle`:
  - TlsDisabled                 (tick 333)
  - MissingCertPath             (tick 333)
  - MissingKeyPath              (tick 333)
  - Io { path, source }         (tick 335)
  - InvalidMinTlsVersion        (tick 335)
  - EmptyCertChain              (this tick)
  - EmptyPrivateKey             (this tick)
Uncovered: MissingClientCaPath (requires mTLS config), Config (rustls-
internal errors not practical to provoke).

Test result: 2 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 21s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
